exemplar.title: Mathematical Analysis of the Rate of the Puffing of Spaghetti Noodles Over Time

detailedExemplarData.title: Mathematical Analysis of the Rate of the Puffing of Spaghetti Noodles Over Time

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear overall structure with introduction, body and conclusion facilitating reader navigation
Logical development of ideas and smooth transitions between sections
Use of headings and numbered tables/figures enhances organization
weaknesses: Occasional lapses in coherence due to typos in equations interrupt flow
Figure captions are truncated and lack full context
Inconsistent citation formatting and missing explicit research question reduce conciseness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place coherence and organization at band 3 and conciseness at band 2. The exploration is well organized and logically developed with clear sections, supporting a score of 3. Minor presentation glitches (typos, incomplete captions, citation inconsistencies) prevent it reaching the top band.

strengths: Effective use of multiple representations (tables, scatter plots, polynomial trendlines) to support analysis
Photographic evidence of methodology complements mathematical discussion
Definition of key variables (e.g., âpuffingâ) and sound derivation of turningâpoint rule
weaknesses: Inconsistent mathematical notation (exponents mis-typed, Ï formatting errors)
Omission of units in tables and SA:V calculations reduces dimensional rigour
Algebraic working occasionally truncated with transcription mistakes
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical language and notation are used occasionally but inconsistently (band 2), multiple representations are effective (band 3), and logical clarity is moderate (band 2). Overall performance aligns with band 2, justifying a score of 2.

strengths: Design and execution of a home experiment demonstrate genuine independent thinking
Personal narrative grounds the exploration in authentic experience
Good creativity shown in selecting variables and justifying polynomial degree
weaknesses: Methodology remains largely within conventional techniques
Personal approach, while clear, does not introduce highly innovative elements
Initiative is solid but not at an exceptional level
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands (independent thinking, personal approach, creativity) fall into band 2: significant evidence of engagement and originality, though not outstanding. Thus, a score of 2 is appropriate.

strengths: Clear evaluation of practical outcomes with suggestions for future improvements
Meaningful reflection on experimental errors and model limitations
Thoughtful analysis of SA:V ratio and its implications
weaknesses: Reflection lacks quantitative uncertainty analysis and deeper statistical critique
Critical analysis of derivative results remains qualitative rather than insightful
Limited forward-looking appraisal of methodological choices
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection strands are consistently at band 2: meaningful reflection, basic critical analysis, and clear evaluation of outcomes. The student shows solid reflection but not the comprehensive depth required for band 3, warranting a score of 2.

strengths: Relevant use of polynomial regression and calculus in addressing the research question
Robust data collection and appropriate application of differentiation and SA:V comparison
Demonstration of conceptual understanding in interpreting turning points and model choice
weaknesses: Transcription and scaling errors in coefficients undermine accuracy
Omission of RÂ² values and measurement uncertainty limits depth of quantitative justification
Techniques, while appropriate, remain at SL rather than demonstrating higher-order sophistication
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Relevance of mathematics meets band 3, level appropriateness and understanding around band 2. Overall, the use of mathematics is appropriate and effective but contains errors and lacks HL-level depth, aligning with a mid-band score of 3.

strandSelections.reasoning: Reasoning is generally clear â the reader sees how data lead to a model and how derivatives are obtained â yet algebraic working is occasionally truncated or contains transcription mistakes, and the discussion of related rates is left un-simplified. These weaknesses lower clarity to the âsome logical structureâ band.

The student demonstrates understanding of polynomial behaviour and basic calculus, yet transcription/rounding errors, incorrect coefficient scaling and an un-simplified related-rates expression indicate partial accuracy. These issues place the work in âsome understanding with errorsâ (band 2) rather than higher bands.

The student provides a table of raw data, multiple scatter plots, polynomial trendlines and a comparative surface-area/volume calculation. These representations link effectively to the discussion and help the reader visualise behaviour, satisfying the descriptor for âeffective use of different formsâ.

The exploration follows a clear sequence â introduction, data collection, model selection, analysis and conclusion â and the reader can follow how each part builds on the previous one. Transitions between sections are generally smooth and the narrative explains why choices (e.g., the 6-degree fit) are made. Minor lapses in logic (e.g., radius equation typos) interrupt the flow but do not break overall coherence, so the work merits the band for a coherent and logically developed piece rather than âexcellent.â

Using everyday equipment, creating a time-series data set and exploring derivative behaviour show creativity and initiative beyond a template exercise, yet they remain within familiar techniques, fitting âgood level of creativityâ (band 2).

There is thoughtful analysis of the RÂ² statistic, turning-point capacity of polynomials and the impact of SA:V ratio. However, derivative results are not interpreted quantitatively and accuracy issues remain unexamined, keeping the analysis in band 2 rather than âdeep and insightfulâ.

The conclusion links the model to practical cooking use and notes future improvements in sample size and instrumentation, representing a clear evaluation with implications but not the comprehensive, forward-looking appraisal of band 3.

The student reflects on experimental errors (cooling time, unequal lengths) and acknowledges that the 6-degree fit is not perfect. This demonstrates meaningful reflection, though depth is not sustained throughout, so band 2 is appropriate.

Key mathematical terms (radius, derivative, RÂ², turning point) and symbols are employed, yet notation is sometimes inconsistent: exponents are mis-typed, units are omitted, and Ï is not typeset consistently. Such lapses indicate âsome appropriate mathematical languageâ, but not the mostly consistent accuracy required for band 3.

The exploration is driven by the studentâs personal cooking interest and decisions (choice of definition of puffiness, radius vs. volume). This demonstrates a clear personal approach but not an innovative methodology, aligning with band 2.

The report is lengthy and occasionally repetitive (e.g., prolonged discussion comparing 4th- and 6th-degree fits, extensive narrative about sampling issues). Most material is relevant, but superfluous anecdotes and duplicated equations reduce conciseness, placing the work in the âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elementsâ band.

The mathematics (polynomial regression, differentiation, related-rates combination, SA:V comparison) is relevant and used appropriately to address the research question. While not sophisticated, it goes beyond simple statistics and applies calculus meaningfully, satisfying band 3 for relevance.

Designing and carrying out a home experiment, justifying the polynomial degree, and critiquing sampling choices show clear independent thinking, though the mathematics remains conventional. The work meets âsignificant evidenceâ rather than âoutstandingâ.

Headings, numbered tables/figures and a recognisable introduction-body-conclusion structure are present. Although some figure captions are truncated and images mis-aligned, the general organisation allows the reader to locate data, working and reflections quickly. These strengths outweigh the formatting glitches, justifying the âwell organised with clear structureâ band rather than the top band.

Techniques employed (polynomial fitting supplied by software, basic differentiation) are mostly at SL level; their application to a real context raises them slightly, but the work does not show full HL-level depth such as analytic model development or numerical methods. Hence âmostly at course levelâ (band 2).

annotations.comment: Photographic evidence of the experimental setup complements the text and provides multiple representations of methodology, enhancing clarity.
The photograph captions lack a scale or reference object. Add a ruler or scale bar to images to allow quantitative assessment of size.
Data are collected at consistent 15 s intervals for a full 8 min, providing a robust dataset for modeling.
The studentâs personal narrative effectively demonstrates genuine interest in the topic, enhancing engagement and grounding the exploration in authentic experience.
The introduction contains lengthy anecdotal detail that distracts from the central focus. Consider streamlining personal commentary and honing in on the research question sooner.
The exploration lacks an explicit statement of the research question. Conclude the introduction by clearly posing the mathematical question to guide the reader.
The definition of âpuffingâ is clearly stated, establishing a precise variable for modeling and ensuring consistent interpretation.
The description of data collection omits any mention of measurement uncertainty or instrument calibration, which is crucial for assessing data reliability.
Include a brief discussion of the caliperâs precision (e.g., Â±0.01 mm) and how you accounted for variability to strengthen the analysis.
The table header omits units (e.g., mm for width). Add explicit units in headings to avoid any ambiguity.
Consider including measurement uncertainties alongside each data point to support later error analysis.
Graph captions are truncated and do not fully explain the axes or context, which hinders comprehension.
Revise all figure captions to include figure number, title, axis labels and units for complete self-contained descriptions.
The identification of the anomalous decrease at 90 s shows critical attention to data integrity.
The selection of a 6th degree polynomial based on visual fit and mention of RÂ² is appropriate.
Although RÂ² is invoked, the actual numerical RÂ² values for 3rd, 4th and 6th degree fits are omitted, weakening quantitative justification.
Include a table of RÂ² values for each polynomial degree to support your model choice with clear numerical evidence.
The explanation linking fewer turning points in a 4th degree polynomial to poorer fit demonstrates solid conceptual understanding.
The polynomial coefficients are presented with inconsistent significant figures and extremely small decimals, making interpretation difficult.
Standardise coefficient notation using scientific format and consistent rounding to improve readability of polynomial equations.
The derivation of the turningâpoint rule using the fundamental theorem is mathematically sound and well-explained.
The text refers to the fundamental theorem without an explicit in-text citation. Include a clear reference to the source immediately in the discussion.
Evaluation of logarithmic and moving-average trendlines demonstrates good initiative in considering alternative models.
Alternative-model graphs lack labels and captions explaining their construction or key features, leaving their evaluation vague.
Add captions and brief commentary for each alternative model graph to clarify how they were generated and why they were rejected.
The polynomial for diameter is misprinted as âx6â rather than âx^6â in the trendline equation, which could lead to misinterpretation.
Ensure correct exponent notation (e.g., x^6) throughout to maintain precision in mathematical communication.
Dividing the polynomial by 2 to obtain r introduces coefficient scaling errors; the resulting coefficients appear incorrect in magnitude.
Recompute the radius function after halving the diameter carefully and verify coefficients to ensure accuracy.
The SA:V ratio comparison shows meaningful reflection on sample limitations and future improvements.
Units are ignored when computing the SA:V ratios, reducing dimensional rigor. Including units or non-dimensionalizing explicitly would strengthen the argument.
Discuss how ignoring units affects the interpretation of SA:V ratios and propose a dimensional analysis to support your reflection.
The discussion of pros and cons for using cross-sectional area versus volume shows thoughtful consideration, but remains qualitative.
Include quantitative comparisons (e.g., percent change) between volume and area models to deepen the reflection on your choice.
Deriving A=Ï[f(t)]^2 clearly shows how the model links time to puffiness through area, demonstrating coherent mathematical use.
The squared polynomial expression for A(t) is unwieldy and difficult to interpret; readers may lose sight of its practical meaning.
Consider simplifying the area function through symbolic software or providing a graph of A(t) to illustrate its behavior.
The differentiation steps for dA/dr and dA/dt are presented succinctly but formatting errors in derivative notation reduce clarity.
Present a fully worked differentiation with aligned steps and clear d/dt and d/dr notation to improve reading flow.
Can you use critical points of dr/dt to identify when puffing is fastest or slows? This would provide practical insight for cooking times.
Extend the analysis by setting dr/dt=0 to find turning points and discuss their relevance to optimal cooking intervals.
The conclusion effectively links the mathematical model to practical noodle cooking and outlines next steps for taste testing.
The reflection on experimental errors is descriptive but lacks quantified uncertainty analysis, limiting depth of evaluation.
In future iterations, quantify margin of error and apply basic statistical analysis (e.g., confidence intervals) to strengthen your reflection.
References appear in multiple formats and lack consistency. A unified citation style would improve presentation professionalism.
Adopt a standard citation style (e.g., MLA or APA) and apply it uniformly across all sources for better consistency.
